Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Plaintiffs' First Amended Complaint Pursuant To Ccp Section 583.210, Et Seq
Off Calendar Mar-04-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT HOLMES US' (F/K/A HOLMES STRUCTURES), ERRONEOUSLY SUED AS HOLMES GROUP (US) INC., Motion To Dismiss Plaintiffs' First Amended Complaint Pursuant To Ccp Section 583.210, Et Seq.

Defendant Holmes US's motion to dismiss all claims alleged against it in plaintiffs' first amended complaint is granted. The undisputed evidence shows that plaintiffs allege that Holmes US is the true name for the fictitiously named Doe 80, Doe 80 is sued in the complaint filed October 6, 2022, no new claims were alleged against Doe 80 in the first amended complaint, and US Holmes was not served with a summons or complaint until December 1, 2025. These facts entitle US Holmes to a dismissal of all claims alleged against it in plaintiffs' first amended complaint per CCP 583.210 and 583.250. All of plaintiffs' arguments in their opposition memorandum lack merit. The mandatory three-year period for service begins with filing of the initial complaint. Where as here, an amended complaint does not add any new claims against the defendant, the filing of the amended complaint does not extend the three-year period. Even assuming that the court should have acted more expeditiously in rejecting plaintiffs' initial effort to file an amendment to identify US Holmes as the true name for Doe 80, the court's delay is not a basis to toll or extend the three-year period. Even assuming that the statute of limitations has not run as to one or more claims against US Holmes, that is not a basis to toll or extend the three-year period. All claims alleged against US Holmes and Doe 80 are dismissed without prejudice.

Proposed order submitted.

Visiting Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/HEK)

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Mar-14-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. JOHN KAZANOVICZ, Esq. of Miller, Morton, Caillat & Nevis, LLP (tel: 408-292-1765) for Plaintiffs.
Atty. CAROLINE FAWLEY, Esq. of Law Offices of Adrienne D. Cohen (tel: 415-761-8655) for Cross-Defendant Rollamatic Roofs, Inc.

HEaring Re: DEFENDANT ROLLAMATIC ROOFS, INC.'s MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Specially appearing party Rollamatic Roofs, Inc. has moved to quash service of summons on it based on an alleged unreasonable delay in identifying it as a Doe defendant in this ongoing case. The court denies the motion.

Leave to amend to name a previously-unknown Doe defendant may be denied, and a motion to quash granted, where the plaintiff has unreasonably delayed the amendment and where prejudice is shown. The court previously continued the hearing and ordered Plaintiffs to supply facts about whether destructive testing of the construction in dispute had occurred. Plaintiffs have done so. The court now finds that there is no unreasonable delay and no prejudice in light of Plaintiffs' showing that destructive testing has not occurred, that discovery is ongoing, and that no trial date has been set.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling, with a cover letter reflecting compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Feb-04-2025 Continued To Mar-14-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT ROLLAMATIC ROOFS, INC.'s MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as modified.

The motion to quash service of summons on Rollamatic Roofs, Inc. is continued to March 14, 2025 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 301. The parties are ordered to complete further briefing as discussed below.

This case arises from a construction dispute that involves waterproofing issues. Plaintiffs knew that Rollamatic designed, constructed, and installed a skylight in the property. They commenced litigation against other entities involved in the construction in October 2022 but did not sue Rollamatic. Plaintiffs' evidence establishes that they became aware that Rollamatic had potential liability "[d]uring further repairs in 2024." Kazanovicz Dec. para. 10. They sued Rollamatic in late 2024. Meet and confer letters presented in Rollamatic's declarations appear to establish that some repairs were taking place around the skylight in early 2024. Declarations from both parties do not establish what repairs took place.

A plaintiff's leave to amend and name Doe defendants is liberally construed, but a motion to quash service of summons may be denied where the newly named defendant would be prejudiced by delay. See A&N v. County of Los Angeles (2009) 171 Cal.App.4th 1058. The court does not find persuasive Rollamatic's argument that its motion should be granted merely because Plaintiffs did not name it sooner. But Rollamatic also raises an argument about prejudice, and specifically that destructive testing of the skylight it installed has occurred. As noted above, the court cannot ascertain from the papers what has occurred with the skylight in 2024 or whether Rollamatic is correct. The burden lies with a defendant to show prejudice, but here access to the facts about what destructive testing if any has occurred is exclusively in Plaintiffs' possession. The court therefore orders Plaintiffs to file a supplemental brief of no more than 10 pages with evidence related to this issue within 21 calendar days of notice of entry of this order. Rollamatic may respond in a brief of no more than 10 pages within 7 calendar days of Plaintiffs' supplemental filing. The matter is continued for hearing to March 14, 2025 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 301.

Order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
